out_of_scope_files.reason: Shell hook implementation. Prompt-verbosity rules ('rules not stories', ## Philosophy / ## Overview / ## Quality Standards / ## Safety Features / ## Helper Scripts / ## Usage section detection) apply to markdown command/agent documentation under commands/*.md and agents/*.md, not to executable safety hooks. The file contains zero markdown verbose-section headers. Severity thresholds (50/100/200 lines) are calibrated for ~600-line markdown command docs and would be a category error if applied to a 1233-line bash safety hook with 141 lines of new security predicates + inline rule-comments + operator-diagnostic stderr messages.

content_classification.executable_predicates: Compound-shape detection, allowlist gates (bare-official-writer, single-pure-read), write-surface vetoes (xargs, find -exec/-delete, etc). Operational bash code.
content_classification.inline_rule_comments: Each comment names a concrete predicate tied 1:1 to the next 3-4 lines of grep (e.g. '# Shell control structure: ; && || | & |& $( ` <( >( << <<<'). Rules, not stories.
content_classification.header_spec_block_lines_633_661: 28-line rule-table enumerating WHOLE-COMMAND-shape allowlist contract (bare-writer / single-pure-read branches + compound denylist). Maps to executable predicates 30 lines below. Acceptable per 'rules not stories' — borderline cycle-history sentence is operator/maintainer context, not prompt-doc verbosity (codex observation_only, not flagged).
content_classification.blocked_stderr_messages: Operator-facing error messages naming the rule, the offending command, the policy reason, and the safe alternative. Part of the rule surface (incident-response interface), not commentary on it.

summary.estimated_reduction: N/A — no prompt-bearing markdown files in this diff

verdict.rationale: Zero verbosity violations introduced in this diff. The only changed file (hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh) is a bash safety hook, out of scope for prompt-verbosity inspection. The +141/-32 diff is intentional security expansion with explicit rules (user-declared), comprising executable predicates + 1:1-mapped rule-comments + operator-diagnostic stderr messages — all part of the rule surface, not narrative verbosity. Per AC-2.6 default-safe rule, file-level findings without an explicit positive new-in-this-diff marker would be advisory; here there are zero findings of any kind.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex (gpt-5.5, xhigh) confirmed the zero-findings verdict on all 5 challenge questions. Q1: no missed verbosity in inline comments — they map 1:1 to predicates. Q2: BLOCKED stderr messages are justified operator error context, not verbose fluff. Q3: severity thresholds (50/100/200) are a category error for a 1233-line bash hook — report as out-of-scope, NOT capability_unavailable (the capability exists, the file is just out of scope). Q4: header block lines 633-661 includes a borderline 'this cycle...' narrative sentence but the block as a whole defines the security invariant — OBSERVATION_ONLY, not flagged. Q5: no false negatives found.

findings.issue: Reporting-scope framing — initial draft phrased the file as 'scored to zero'; should be framed as out-of-scope for prompt-verbosity inspection
findings.proposed_fix: Keep findings:[], set files_inspected:0, list hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh under out_of_scope_files with reason 'Shell hook implementation; prompt-verbosity rules apply to markdown command/agent documentation, not executable safety hooks.' Do NOT use capability_unavailable — the capability exists, this file is just out of scope.
findings.rationale: Codex's framing is correct — applying markdown-doc verbosity thresholds to a bash hook is a category error. The final report now uses out_of_scope_files (not findings, not capability_unavailable) and sets files_inspected:0.

findings.issue: No missed verbosity violation in inline Layer 1.F comments
findings.proposed_fix: Keep zero findings for inline comments.

findings.issue: Header block lines 633-661 contains one borderline narrative sentence ('This cycle (20260525-095242) tightens...') but block as a whole is the security invariant spec
findings.rationale: Codex explicitly marked OBSERVATION_ONLY — 'could be shortened, but should not be emitted as a prompt-verbosity finding'. Logged here; not raised as a finding. Functional operator/maintainer context in a safety hook is not the same as ## Philosophy filler in a markdown doc.

findings.issue: BLOCKED stderr messages (8+ lines each) are justified operator error context
findings.proposed_fix: Keep zero findings for the stderr block.

findings.issue: Severity thresholds are a category error for this file
findings.proposed_fix: Do not calculate verbosity_percentage/severity for this file; represent as out-of-scope with findings:[].
findings.rationale: Final report does not compute any verbosity_percentage or severity for hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh. The file appears only under out_of_scope_files with diff_analysis context.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Two applied (out_of_scope reframing + no severity calculation) and three observation_only/confirmation logged. Final report carries zero findings, files_inspected:0, blocks_close:false.
